preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Saas founders seeking an AI-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for SaaS founders evaluating AI-ready design systems. At decision stage, the Validator archetype needs to quickly confirm they're in the right place and that MODE addresses their specific need — an AI-ready design system built for SaaS. Sets the frame before any persuasion begins.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: With orientation set, the Validator needs to see concrete capabilities and outcome promises before they'll engage with proof. This beat articulates what MODE delivers — AI-readiness, intent-aware components, SaaS-specific patterns — giving the founder a clear mental model of the product's value proposition to validate against their requirements.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators demand third-party validation before they trust claims. Placing credibility after value lets the social proof — customer logos, founder testimonials, recognized SaaS brands using MODE — directly reinforce the capabilities just presented. This bridges the gap between 'what you say you do' and 'who already trusts you.'

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required for decision-stage Validators. Hard metrics, case study data, and quantified outcomes (e.g., time-to-ship reduction, design consistency scores, adoption rates) provide the empirical proof this archetype needs. Positioned after credibility so the numbers feel grounded in real customer context rather than abstract.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at decision funnel stage. The founder is now primed with value, trust, and proof — this beat presents pricing tiers, plan comparisons, and feature breakdowns that frame the final choice. For Validators, transparent plan structure eliminates ambiguity and reduces perceived risk of commitment.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately precedes Recovery as required. With all validation satisfied — capabilities confirmed, credibility established, evidence reviewed, and pricing understood — this beat delivers a confident, friction-low CTA to start a paid plan. The commitment prompt can reference the proof points to reinforce decision confidence at the moment of action.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat as required. Provides secondary paths for Validators who need one more loop — links to documentation, a detailed feature comparison page, or a 'talk to our team' option. Ensures no decision-stage visitor exits without an alternative path back into the funnel.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Footer

page.reasoning: Decision-stage Validators need conversion focus. Notes specify: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' This aligns with the decision funnel stage.

Validator archetype requires trust signals at page opening. Notes state: 'Validator variant always includes trust_signal.' with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster for credibility.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The intent-aware intelligence layer for SaaS design systems. Components that resolve behavior to the right archetype, funnel stage, and context — without manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validators need outcome-oriented clarity. Notes specify: 'Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements.' 3-col-icon is the default and supports this articulation.

slots.headline: Built for the moment AI made design systems dumb.
slots.subhead: MODE adds the semantic layer that tells your components who they're talking to, where in the funnel they are, and how to behave accordingly.

features.title: Semantic Resolution Engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across four dimensions — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — without a single manual override.

features.title: IA Planner
features.description: Go from brief to full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus, wired to your brand tokens from the first run.

features.title: Multi-Variant Generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Test three positioned experiences without writing three briefs.

features.title: Palette Map
features.description: Visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage automatically. No manual color assignment. Semantic weight accumulates toward conversion.

features.title: Component Manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Built on Radix UI — nothing new to learn.

features.title: Full Ownership
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase. No runtime dependency, no vendor dashboard, no usage-based billing. Fork it, extend it, ship it.

page.reasoning: Selection rules prioritize variants with 'social-proof' for Validators. Notes confirm: 'Validator variant leads with quote, logos below.' This dual-signal approach maximizes credibility at decision stage.

slots.headline: Trusted by SaaS founders who ship in code.
slots.quote: We'd been patching our design system with one-off variants for every campaign. MODE replaced that entire layer. The resolution engine alone saved us weeks of QA on our last funnel build.
slots.attribution: Early access founder, B2B SaaS — 2024 beta cohort

page.reasoning: Selection rules require 'with-source' for Validators. Notes state: 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.' This is critical for decision-stage conversion.

slots.headline: The numbers behind the intelligence layer.

stats.label: Median time from install to first working brief output
Faster page variant production vs. manual design system configuration
Components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and behavioral tokens — on day one

page.reasoning: Decision-stage Validators need clear recommendation. Notes indicate: 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' and 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list' to support informed purchase decisions.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, IA planner, multi-variant generation, palette map, and Next.js preview UI — in your codebase, under your control.
features: Three palette presets — funnel, archetype, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map
Lifetime access — no subscription, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust signals in conversion moments. Notes specify: 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required').' Centered layout maximizes focus for terminal actions.

slots.headline: The semantic layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: MODE is running in under an hour. Configure once, and every component in your system gains the judgment it's never had.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. No subscription. No runtime dependency. Bring your own Anthropic API key.

page.reasoning: At decision stage on conversion pages, recovery is critical. Notes state: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.' This prevents abandonment during final consideration.
